acceptance.proof: The public native harness reports successful explicit-interface join, drop, and rejoin on the exact group within a finite deadline.
One Quest requester receives the exact bounded response from an independently running Quest peer after multicast query transmission; same-process loopback is not used.

A cancellation probe closes owned sockets before its deadline, both target packages stop, app data and run-owned staging are removed, no forwards/reverses or properties remain, and the bounded target-package fatal window is empty.
Sanitized hash-bound evidence, exact source heads, Standard policy, public-boundary, documentation/instruction, and workflow gates preserve Rusty LSL, Quest, and Manifold ownership boundaries.

commit_policy: Commit lifecycle separately; commit the isolated Rusty Quest harness before locking exact source composition; publish only sanitized Rusty LSL device evidence after complete cleanup and validation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the bounded Quest-only compatibility evidence and its nonclaims.
Route the device-conformance result separately from production support.
Record sanitized exact-head Quest evidence and limitations.
Document the public native Android harness, CLI inputs, markers, and lane boundary.
Review whether the unit changes durable device policy; retain unchanged when existing serial-scoped and cleanup rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill against the run capsule.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Existing cross-repository authority and evidence guidance covers this bounded platform harness.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed against the Rusty LSL, Quest, and Manifold authority split.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Existing Rusty Morphospace lane routing covers public Rusty LSL evidence and Quest packaging ownership.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Reviewed against the public/private and cross-repository boundary.

non_scope: Production Rusty LSL API/runtime/activation changes; groups other than 239.255.172.215:16571; IPv6; interface enumeration or default-selection policy; background services; Makepad; devices other than the selected Quest 3S pair; dependencies in Rusty LSL; unsafe/FFI or copied liblsl source; stream admission, routing, commands, or Manifold authority.
objective: Validate the accepted Rusty LSL exact IPv4 multicast discovery envelope on Quest 3S with one purpose-built native Android test harness: explicit interface, bounded join/drop/rejoin, one query/response across two devices, deadline/cancellation, effective marker, target-package fatal scan, and complete run-owned cleanup without adding production runtime or Manifold authority.
outputs: One small public native Android/CLI-testable multicast conformance harness owned by Rusty Quest packaging.
Private content-addressed build and two-device run capsule with raw logs, serials, APK, endpoints, diagnostics, and cleanup receipt.
One sanitized hash-bound Rusty LSL compatibility fixture/checker documenting exact heads, pass dimensions, limitations, and authority nonclaims.

validation.command: Run the private serial-scoped LSLC-004G two-Quest capsule and sanitize through tools/check_lslc_004g.ps1.
